The `tsconfig.json` includes `tui/src` at line 21. The `tsc` in the build command (`tsc && vite build`) uses this config and tries to compile TUI files, but `ink`/`@inkjs/ui`/`eventsource` are only in the TUI's own package.json.

The fix: split the tsconfig so the Vite build only checks `src` and `shared`, while TUI has its own config. Let me check the TUI setup.

content.text:
The root `tsconfig.json` includes `tui/src` which causes `tsc` (run during `npm run build`) to try compiling TUI files. The TUI has its own tsconfig that extends the root.

The fix: remove `tui/src` from the root `tsconfig.json` include. The TUI has its own `tui/tsconfig.json` for its builds. The root config should only cover the Vite/Tauri app (`src` and `shared`).

Everything passes. The fix is a one-line change in `tsconfig.json`: remove `tui/src` from the `include` array. 

The root `tsc` (used by `npm run build` and `npm run check`) was trying to compile TUI source files that depend on `ink`, `@inkjs/ui`, and `eventsource` — packages only installed in the `tui/` sub-package. The TUI already has its own `tui/tsconfig.json` for its separate build pipeline, so it doesn't need to be in the root config.
